rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use visible identifiers when referring to component ports in AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure when inserting annex blocks
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to AADL hierarchy
Use visible identifiers for component ports in AGREE contracts
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings like assignments
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, equations, and assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid scopes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax for assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign
AADL property names and their usage in AGREE expressions
Type scope range assumption and guarantee patterns
Type scope deterministic dependency guarantee patterns
